commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Weather icons lack aria-labels or decorative alt tags.
issues.fix: Add aria-hidden='true' to icon elements or provide descriptive labels for screen readers.

issues.description: The component is static; no hover states or interactions on forecast items.
issues.fix: Wrap forecast items in a button or interactive container to provide visual feedback on hover.

issues.description: The vertical divider in the details strip lacks a defined height in some design systems.
issues.fix: Explicitly define the height of the divider or wrap it in a container with a fixed height.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and follows design system principles effectively. It covers all requirements clearly and uses a clean, maintainable layout. Minor enhancements to screen reader support and interactive states would elevate it to production-ready status.

evaluation.critique: The component has good hierarchy and data presentation, but significantly undermines its design system compliance by falling back to hardcoded hex colors (#6b7280, #f3f4f6) instead of using ggui tokens consistently. The layout is functional but lacks responsive design considerations (fixed maxWidth, no mobile adaptation). Missing interactive states and transitions expected in modern weather cards.
